title: Encouraging, They Said

mood.label: Uneasy calm
mood.summary: A day that leaned just barely toward de-escalation — Switzerland talks called 'encouraging' and oil easing — but a Hormuz standoff, a climbing Ebola count and a widening Sudan war kept it pinned near neutral.

style.genre: chamber-pop / acoustic small-ensemble — pizzicato string ensemble and a sampled marimba trading a circling figure over a soft brushed RhythmAce kit, a plucked upright bass and a low clarinet line, with a warm bowed-string pad and high oboe glints underneath; Bb mixolydian's lowered seventh keeping the brightness honest and unsettled, neither rising nor sinking

sampleSources: Strudel default library (VCSL pizzicato strings, marimba, clarinet, oboe, string ensemble, acoustic bass, RhythmAce)
pattern:
stack(
  s("~ rim ~ rim").bank("RhythmAce").gain(0.3).room(0.24),
  s("hh*8").bank("RhythmAce").gain(perlin.range(0.04,0.12).slow(9)).pan(0.6),
  s("~ ~ ~ sd").bank("RhythmAce").gain(0.18).room(0.3),
  note("<[bf2 ~ f3 ~] [ef3 ~ bf3 ~] [g2 ~ d3 ~] [f2 ~ c3 ~]>").s("gm_pizzicato_strings").gain(0.4).lpf(2200).room(0.3).off(0.125, x => x.gain(0.18).pan(0.7)),
  note("<[bf4 c5 d5] [f5 ~ ef5] [d5 c5 bf4] [c5 ~ f4]>").s("marimba").gain(0.34).lpf(sine.range(1800,3600).slow(12)).delay(0.22).delaytime(0.3).room(0.32).off(0.25, x => x.gain(0.14).pan(0.32)),
  note("<[bf2,d3,f3] [ef3,g3,bf3] [g2,bf2,d3] [f2,a2,c3]>").s("gm_string_ensemble_1").attack(0.9).release(2.3).sustain(0.8).gain(0.2).lpf(sine.range(650,1400).slow(16)).room(0.5).slow(2),
  note("<bf1 ef2 g1 f1>").s("gm_acoustic_bass").gain(0.42).lpf(500).release(0.6).slow(2),
  note("<[~ bf3 d4 ~] [~ c4 ~ ef4] [~ d4 bf3 ~] [~ c4 ~ a3]>").s("gm_clarinet").gain(0.22).attack(0.08).release(0.5).lpf(1600).room(0.34).pan(0.42),
  note("~ ~ <d6 f6> ~").s("oboe").gain(0.14).room(0.55).delay(0.3).delaytime(0.4).pan(0.55)
)

lines: They called it encouraging in a Swiss hotel, a committee and a sixty-day map,
then a delegation stood and walked the floor at the word hit hard again, the trap.
The strait stays closed in protest, or it's open — both sides swear it's so,
and oil reads the room near seventy-seven, easing slow, not letting go.
So call it uneasy calm, a half-light holding the line,
the chord won't lift to bright, won't fall to minor — just a lowered seventh sign.
We carry the table and the threat, the roadmap and the worsening ward —
near neutral, breathing careful, leaning a hair toward the word toward.
Eight hundred thirty-seven counted now where Bundibugyo climbs through Congo's heat,
a three-or-four-of-ten response, a strike inside Sudan, a war that won't retreat.
But a trial nearly doubles a year, a vaccine clears its first human test —
Colombia waits on a razor count, and we hold both the worry and the best.
